measured.transcript: We will hear argument this morning in case twenty two four Richard Glossop was convicted on the word of one. man, Justin Sneed, the undisputed murderer in this case. Oklahoma has now disclosed evidence revealing that Mr Sneed lied to the jury about his history of psychiatric treatment, including the fact that a prison psychiatrist prescribed lithium to treat his previously undiagnosed disorder. The prosecution suppressed that evidence and then fail to correct Mr Sneed's perjured denial, just as it suppressed evidence that in the middle of trial, in violation of the Court's sequestration order, Sneed altered his testimony about the knife on the victim at the urgent request of the prosecutor, who then falsely denied to the Court her prior knowledge. There is no adequate or independent jurisdictional bar to review and no warrant for an hearing. As to independence, the opinion is suffused with merit's determination on the Brady and Napo claims, and certainly there is no clear express statement that the Court's is based on a bona separate, adequate and independent grounds as long by this Court to preclude review, is there any adequate bar. By the State's waiver, the Court a jurisdictional threshold it had never in any other case. The disposition was, quote, without support in prior State law, as required over sixty years of this Court's precedent to establish adequacy. No evidentiary hearing could alter the conclusion Mr Glossop was denied due process.
